description_long: This object is a rectangular solar panel array, featuring a grid of photovoltaic cells encased in a dark, likely metallic or composite frame. The surface is divided into two main sections, each containing multiple smaller rectangular cells arranged in a regular pattern. Along one edge, several protruding tabs or brackets are visible, which likely serve as mounting points for installation on a flat or slightly inclined surface. The panel's design is low-profile and flat, optimized for maximum sun exposure in urban environments such as rooftops, parking structures, or ground installations. The frame appears robust, suggesting durability against weather and environmental factors. No visible electrical components or cabling are present on the exterior, indicating a clean and integrated design.
description: A flat, rectangular solar panel array with a grid of photovoltaic cells and mounting brackets along one edge, encased in a dark, sturdy frame.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full rectangular grid of photovoltaic cells and the mounting brackets along the top edge.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the panel and the protruding mounting brackets along the side.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually similar to the front, displaying the grid pattern and mounting brackets, but with less detail on the cell surfaces.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the slim profile and the arrangement of mounting brackets.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted or flat-roof-mounted with metal brackets
attribute_array_configuration: single row of 2 large rectangular panels arranged side by side
